standard.name: Shrine
standard.desc: A stone altar to an unnamed god. Praying grants advantage on the next saving throw (one creature, once per long rest).

standard.name: Armory
standard.desc: Weapon racks with mundane arms. 2d6 javelins, 1d4 shields, a rusted longsword, a bundle of crossbow bolts.

standard.name: Barracks
standard.desc: Crude sleeping quarters. 1d4 locked footlockers (DC 13), 2d4 straw pallets, a crudely drawn guard schedule.

standard.name: Torture Chamber
standard.desc: Iron maiden, rack, and brazier. A key hangs on the wall (opens a random locked door in the dungeon).

standard.name: Prison Cell
standard.desc: Three iron-barred cells. One contains a skeletal prisoner clutching a water-damaged journal with dungeon notes.

standard.name: Kitchen
standard.desc: Cold hearth, rotten provisions. A hidden compartment (DC 14 Investigation) holds preserved rations and 15 gp.

standard.name: Trophy Room
standard.desc: Mounted monster heads and adventurer skulls. Searching finds a non-magical weapon of fine quality (+1 to hit, no magic).

standard.name: Guard Post
standard.desc: Murder holes overlook the corridor. Creatures defending here have three-quarters cover against ranged attacks.

standard.name: Library
standard.desc: Shelves of crumbling tomes. DC 15 Investigation: one random spell scroll (1st–3rd level).

standard.name: Forge
standard.desc: Cold forge and bellows. With 1 hour of work, a skilled smith (DC 15 tools) can repair a broken magic weapon.

standard.name: Map Room
standard.desc: A rough map of the dungeon is scratched into the wall. DM reveals the layout to players.

standard.name: Scrying Pool
standard.desc: A still basin of black water. DC 14 Arcana to use as a scrying sensor (1 min, concentration).

undead.name: Crypt
undead.desc: Row of stone sarcophagi sealed with wax. Breaking any has a 50% chance to release a Ghoul.

undead.name: Bone Yard
undead.desc: Bones piled floor to ceiling. Moving through costs a full action per 5 ft. DC 12 STR or gain one level of exhaustion.

undead.name: Soul Well
undead.desc: A pit radiating negative energy. Undead within 30 ft heal 1d4 HP/turn. Living must pass DC 13 CON or gain one exhaustion level.

undead.name: Vampire's Sanctum
undead.desc: Silk-draped coffin, ornate mirror (shows nothing). 1d4 Vampire Spawn lurk in the shadows.

undead.name: Mausoleum Shrine
undead.desc: Altar to Myrkul. A cleric can cast Speak with Dead without components once per day here.

arcane.name: Wizard's Study
arcane.desc: Desk with half-completed spell research. DC 14 Arcana: one random 1st-level spell scroll discovered in the drawer.

arcane.name: Alchemy Lab
arcane.desc: Bubbling alembics. 1d4 random potions on the shelf; 25% chance one is explosive if dropped (2d6 fire, 10 ft radius).

arcane.name: Teleportation Circle
arcane.desc: Permanent circle etched in the floor. Its destination is unknown (DM determines).

arcane.name: Golem Workshop
arcane.desc: Inert stone and clay husks. One incomplete golem can be animated with a spell slot of 6th level or higher.

arcane.name: Spell Mirror
arcane.desc: A 7-ft mirror that reflects spells. First spell targeting a creature before it is redirected at the caster (once per day).

demonic.name: Summoning Circle
demonic.desc: Blood-drawn circle. Still active—any creature stepping inside must pass DC 16 WIS or be compelled to complete the ritual.

demonic.name: Desecrated Altar
demonic.desc: Altar to Orcus, slicked with blood. Undead within 10 ft regain 2d6 HP per round.

demonic.name: Torture Pit
demonic.desc: A 20-ft pit of boiling ichor. DC 15 CON against fumes (1d6 poison/round adjacent; no save in pit).

demonic.name: Idol Chamber
demonic.desc: A 10-ft obsidian statue of a demon lord. Smashing it (DC 18 Athletics) causes 4d10 thunder to all within 30 ft.

goblinoid.name: War Room
goblinoid.desc: Crude tactical maps on animal hides. Studying them grants advantage on one Insight or Perception check about the dungeon.

goblinoid.name: Trophy Pile
goblinoid.desc: Stolen gear from past adventurers. Roll d6: 1–2 mundane item; 3–4 +1 magic item (DM's choice); 5 cursed item; 6 key to locked door.

goblinoid.name: Pen
goblinoid.desc: 1d4 captured humanoids (commoners). They know the rough layout of 1d4 rooms.

goblinoid.name: Fungal Garden
goblinoid.desc: Bioluminescent mushrooms. Three varieties: edible; mildly psychedelic (1 hour); violently poisonous (DC 13 CON or 3d6 poison).

nature.name: Underground Spring
nature.desc: Fresh water source. Drinking heals 1d4 HP (once per creature per day).

nature.name: Druid Grove
nature.desc: Impossible circle of trees underground. Druid spells cast here have doubled duration.

nature.name: Predator Den
nature.desc: Bones of prey animals. A large predator has nested here—tracks suggest it left recently and will return.

nature.name: Crystal Formation
nature.desc: Quartz veins worth 2d6×10 gp if carefully extracted (2 hours work, mining tools required).

underdark.name: Drow Outpost
underdark.desc: Collapsed webbing and a broken hand crossbow. A message in Elvish warns of a Drow patrol arriving in 4 hours.

underdark.name: Aboleth Pool
underdark.desc: A 30-ft underground lake. An Aboleth may lair here. DC 16 Perception to notice the ripples beneath the dark surface.

underdark.name: Faerzress Node
underdark.desc: Wild magic zone. All spell slots are random tier (DM rolls d4 for slot level). Wild magic surges on any d20 roll of 1–10.

underdark.name: Myconid Colony
underdark.desc: Myconids tend to purple mushroom patches. They will trade dungeon information for surface food or 'novel experiences'.

underdark.name: Darkmantle Roost
underdark.desc: Ceiling covered in dormant darkmantles (appear as stalactites). Loud noise or any light source of 20+ ft radius wakes them all.

draconic.name: Hoard Chamber
draconic.desc: Piles of gold coins (see treasure for total). Disturbing the hoard triggers a DC 18 Perception check for any nearby dragon.

draconic.name: Egg Chamber
draconic.desc: 1d4 dragon eggs on a heated stone shelf, carefully arranged. Each is worth 2,500 gp to collectors or dragon cults.

draconic.name: Shrine to Tiamat
draconic.desc: Five-headed altar. Non-evil creatures must pass DC 14 CHA or be frightened for 1 minute on first sight.

draconic.name: Drake Pen
draconic.desc: Guard Drakes (treat as Wyverns without flight) chained here. Chains have 30 HP, AC 17.

fey.name: Faerie Court
fey.desc: A tiny throne of twisted silver. Any creature who sits must pass DC 15 WIS or compulsively perform a minor task for the invisible court.

fey.name: Time Bubble
fey.desc: Time moves differently here. Each hour spent inside ages the creature 1d4 years (reversible with Greater Restoration).

fey.name: Dream Pool
fey.desc: A pool of liquid moonlight. Touching it triggers a vivid vision relevant to the current quest (DM's discretion).

fey.name: Riddle Door
fey.desc: A sealed fey archway that poses a riddle. Correct answer opens it; wrong answer causes 2d8 psychic damage and resets.

elemental_fire.name: Magma Forge
elemental_fire.desc: A forge fed by natural magma. Weapons crafted here gain the 'Heated' property (deals +1d4 fire damage for 1 hour after forging).

elemental_fire.name: Fire Shrine
elemental_fire.desc: Altar to Kossuth. Fire-resistant creatures may commune here to gain immunity to fire for 1 hour (once per day).

elemental_fire.name: Salamander Nest
elemental_fire.desc: A heated burrow. 2d4 salamander eggs are present; each is worth 50 gp to the right buyer.

elemental_cold.name: Ice Vault
elemental_cold.desc: Frozen in the walls are preserved treasures from a previous age. DC 16 Athletics to break open; contents as per treasure table.

elemental_cold.name: Frost Shrine
elemental_cold.desc: Altar of shimmering blue ice. Cold-immune creatures may commune here to gain advantage on CON saves for 24 hours.

elemental_cold.name: Permafrost Cave
elemental_cold.desc: Bodies of explorers perfectly preserved in ice. DC 14 Investigation reveals valuables still on their persons.

infernal.name: Contract Chamber
infernal.desc: Walls lined with infernal contracts bound in flesh. Reading them reveals the fates of previous dungeon occupants.

infernal.name: Soul Cage
infernal.desc: A brass lantern containing a trapped soul. Releasing it grants a favor from the freed spirit; keeping it angers powerful infernal beings.

infernal.name: Devil's Throne
infernal.desc: A throne of black iron. Sitting upon it and speaking a command word summons 1d4 Imps who must obey for 1 hour.

aquatic.name: Sunken Treasury
aquatic.desc: A submerged vault with a pressurized lock (DC 16 Athletics). Treasure inside is wet but intact.

aquatic.name: Tidal Cave
aquatic.desc: Filled and drained by tides every 6 hours. When flooded, the entire room is 10 ft underwater.

aquatic.name: Pearl Grotto
aquatic.desc: Giant clams line the walls. 1d4 contain non-magical pearls worth 50–100 gp each; 1 contains a Pearl of Power.

giant.name: Giant's Larder
giant.desc: A pantry stocked with giant-sized provisions. Among the giant food is an ordinary chest containing 200 gp and a +1 weapon.

giant.name: Trophy Hall
giant.desc: Dragon skulls and hero armor mounted as trophies. One set of armor is still functional (scale mail +1, sized for Medium creatures).

giant.name: Giant Throne
giant.desc: An enormous throne carved from a single boulder. Beneath the seat cushion (DC 12 Investigation) is a giant's personal hoard (500 gp).

construct.name: Control Room
construct.desc: A bank of levers and dials. DC 15 Arcana to understand the controls; success lets you lock/unlock any 2 doors in the dungeon.

construct.name: Golem Storage
construct.desc: Three dormant golems stand in alcoves. They activate if attacked or if anyone moves a stored item without the correct command word.

construct.name: Assembly Floor
construct.desc: Half-assembled automatons on workbenches. DC 14 Investigation finds a functional Clockwork Amulet (+1 to ability checks involving clocks).
